title: ENTER RAA SPACE
description: RAA is a cultural space that hosts regular theatrical and contemporary performances, exhibitions and workshops. At RAA you don’t have to be loud to be heard. A little herbal shop offers the possibility to take in some Latvian forest in the centre of Riga.

telpa.title: Space
telpa.paragraph1: RAA is a cultural space that hosts regular theatrical and contemporary performances, exhibitions and workshops. At RAA you don’t have to be loud to be heard. A little herbal shop offers the possibility to take in some Latvian forest in the centre of Riga.
telpa.paragraph2: Raa is raa. If it was something else it wouldn't be called so. In some fields of inquiry an argument that aims to establish a claim by proving that the contrary would lead to absurdity is called Reductio ad Absurdum, sometimes also abbreviated to RAA.
telpa.layoutDescription: The front-facing part of the space consists of an entrance foyer (35 m^2) and an exhibition hall (103m^2). There are 10 large windows facing the Matisa street, that gives the space its spacious and stage-like feeling. Behind the entrance foyer there is a shop called 'māte nātre' inside of 12m^2 separate room. The backward facing part consists of a long corridor that contains a backstage area (8m^2) and an adjacent toilet (4m^2). Then comes a theatre area with a theatre foyer and a small theatre room (13m^2). At the very end of the corridor there is a storage room (6,4m^2). We also have access to a magical court yard behind the building. From there one can reach all of Veldze quarter.
telpa.mapDescription: You can find us at Matīsa iela 8, Rīga. Just opposite the fire station and the the Vidzeme Market. It is part of the Veldze quarter. The most recognizable landmark is a tall brick chimney that pokes out just behind RAA. The easiest way to reach RAA is via Brivibas street. You take any of the busses or trolley busses and get off at 'Matīsa iela' stop. Then you walk into Matīsa street and you will see RAA on your right after about 150m. You may also reach us via Krišjāņa Barona street. There are two trams that stop at 'Bernu pasaule', namely tram 1 and 11. You can get off at 'Bernu pasaule' stop and walk into Matīsa street. RAA is on your left after about 200m.

series.title: RAA Series
series.description: At RAA we hold several repeating regular events. We call them RAA series. You may visit them either weekly, monthly or yearly, depending on the series.

raaMuseum.title: RAA Museum
raaMuseum.description: RAA Museum is a series of monthly thematic exhibitions, in which mundane objects, and non-objects are presented.

dialogoAdAbsurdum.title: Dialogo Ad Absurdum
dialogoAdAbsurdum.description: Dialogo Ad Absurdum is an performative exploration of  dialogues. The visitor enters into a space where they find out about themselves and about the art of dialogue.

kabiirijasSalons.title: Kabiirijas Salons
kabiirijasSalons.description: Every Thursday of the week the Kàtoptron Teātris (Matīsa 8, RAA) will be open to all -failed or almost failed - artists in Rīga, for free and experimental artistic expression of any kind. From painting and sketching, to open music Jamming, performance, grotesque fashion, dance, poetry etc.

kizomba.title: Kizomba / UrbanKiz Party
kizomba.shortDescription: The hottest autumn dance night in Riga! Kizomba / UrbanKiz party with DJ music, warm vibes and a refreshing non-alcoholic bar. 04.10 at 19:00, RAA.
kizomba.description:
The hottest dance night of the autumn in Riga!
✨ Kizomba / UrbanKiz PARTY by Prima Studio ✨
📅 October 4 | 19:00
📍 RAA, Matīsa Street 8

What to expect:
💃 A warm and welcoming atmosphere
🌆 The charm of the urban environment
🎶 DJ rhythms that will keep you on your feet
🥤 A refreshing non-alcoholic drinks bar

👉 Bring your friends, your love for dance and a smile – we’ll take care of the rest!

Save this post and share it with your friends!

resTacta.title: res tacta — performance / creative workshop
resTacta.shortDescription: An evening of touch, sound, and long-exposure photography: phonographs, piano, interactive experiments, and the forest bar “māte nātre”. 08.10 at 18:30, RAA. Free entry.
resTacta.description:
res tacta. performance / creative workshop

From Latin: “a thing that is touched” or “a tangible thing.”

An object touched by the human hand becomes a link between historical heritage and a living instrument, from which a unique experience is born in the moment of contact — here and now.

The phonograph needle touches the record, the piano hammer vibrates the strings, the camera shutter releases and the curtain opens to capture a moment that becomes tangible.
______________________

The evening will feature the musical performance “All My Phonographs,” where the sounds of phonographs intertwine with piano accompaniment in a long-exposure light aesthetic. Visitors will also be able to explore the history and construction of phonographs and meet collector Sanita Duka.

In the second part of the evening, led by Jevgenija Frolova, everyone is invited to take part in an interactive analogue photography experiment — moving to music, photographing one another with long-exposure settings, and engaging in creative collaboration in the making of images.

Photographer Armands Andže will capture the course of the evening using the dry plate technique with three historic large-format cameras, resulting in three unique long-exposure images from different perspectives.

Later, the photographs from this evening will be exhibited at RAA as a reminder of the fleeting moment of touch that became a tangible memory.

Throughout the event, the forest bar “māte nātre” will be open, offering forest-inspired drinks from the Baltics.

⚪️ Dress code: light-colored clothes, which stand out best in long-exposure photographs.

🎟 Free entry

See you soon!

Line-up:
• Sanita Duka — phonograph collection
• Jevgenija Frolova — analogue photography experiment
• Armands Andže — dry plate photography
• Jana Zariņa — performative piano accompaniment in long exposure

rigaSockExchange.title: Riga Sock Exchange
rigaSockExchange.shortDescription: What happens to socks when they loose their partner? They go to the Riga Sock Exchange with Single Sock Dating, Sock Wrestling and Sock Exchange. Bring your single socks to RAA and join an afternoon of fun: Sock Dating, Sock Wrestling, and the great Sock Exchange.
rigaSockExchange.location: RAA.space, Matīsa Street 8 (opposite fire brigade), Riga
rigaSockExchange.price: Entrance: bring a washed single sock. Donation of 5€ welcome.
rigaSockExchange.description:
Riga finally hosts the event your single socks have all been waiting for: The Riga Sock Exchange!

Last seen in the closet, entwined with its partner, your sock might have been sucked up by a vacuum cleaner, slipped into the lingerie drawer, torn and digested by neighbour's dog, fallen into a sofa crevasse near grandma's teeth or it suffered the classic mysterious laundry fate that has widowed many socks.

If your single sock was anyways bored of its previous partner or it simply looks for a new adventure or something more long term, maybe something out of the ordinary or in fact something quite ordinary. The Riga Sock Exchange is where to go and enjoy a late afternoon of fun with the following program lined up:

- 17h - Sock Dating Show
- 18h - Sock Wrestling (playful sock-game also for sock-owners)
- 19h - Sock Exchange (find other single socks)

You will be guided through the evening by Reinis Ziediņš and Leonhard Horstmeyer. The event will be accompanied by live music. You can enjoy one of our special socktails or some hot or cold beverages from our forest bar "māte nātre".

When: 5th of October, 17h–20h
Where: RAA.space (Matīsa iela 8, opposite of fire brigade)
Entrance: Please bring a washed single sock. Donation of 5€ is welcome.
Registration: Please do register (see link below)

See you soon!

scentArtFestival.title: Scent Art Festival
scentArtFestival.shortDescription: International Scent Art Festival in Riga: exhibition, performances, workshops, and a scent tour (10–12 October). Opening on 10.10 at 18:00.
scentArtFestival.location: RAA / Creative Factory Veldze, Matīsa Street 8, Riga
scentArtFestival.description: International Scent Art Festival – Riga. From October 10–12 at the Creative Factory Veldze, 40 artists from 8 countries explore scent across visual art, music, literature, dance, and performance. Expect: an exhibition of 18 scent-integrated works, live performances blending sound, movement, and smell, aromography workshops and talks on multisensory interaction, plus a special scent tour of Riga. Special guests from Argentina — Maria Zegna, Bruno Mesz, and Sebastian Tedesco — bring their unique smell organ for five performances (including a gastronomic show, a contemporary music piece performed by flutist Lieba Dobīčina, and a video–scent fusion). The exhibition features paintings, prints, installations, and sound pieces including olfactory experience by artists from Norway, Finland, Portugal, New Zealand, China, Greece, and Latvia. Also: scent tours (Solvita Lazdāne), lectures (Jurģis Šķilters), an introduction to aromography (Sandris Mūriņš), and a hands-on workshop (Amanda Nīcmane) showcasing the “Scent Camera”. Official supporter: Creative Factory Veldze. Festival logo by Santa Kristiāna Krūze.

kefirsStudioExhibition.title: Kefirs Studio Exhibition
kefirsStudioExhibition.shortDescription: Kefirs Studio exhibition at RAA (03–09 November).

brivibasExperience.title: Brīvības Experience
brivibasExperience.shortDescription: Pilsetas cilvēkiem presents an exhibition about Riga’s main street – The Brīvības Experience. Six-lane traffic choreography and future visions. October 16–30, RAA.
brivibasExperience.description:
The first and only exhibition dedicated to the unique charm of our city’s main highwa… ahem, street – the expanses of asphalt concrete and the symphony of engines. Visitors will experience the authentic city atmosphere of empty shop windows and the choreography of six-lane traffic.

Using various visualizations, 3D models, and other media, the exhibition highlights the history of Brīvības Street, its current state, and possible scenarios for the future. Visitors will be able to actively take part in rethinking how to make the city more people-friendly.

Organizer: Brunis Kibilds (City for People).
Dates: 16–30 October, RAA.

exoFotoIzstade.title: Exo
exoFotoIzstade.shortDescription: A one-day exhibition exploring the idea of the exoskeleton — not only as a machine around the body, but as extensions of faith, technology, and imagination that help us move through life.
exoFotoIzstade.description:
Where does the human end and the machine begin? Perhaps an exoskeleton is not only metal wrapped around muscles, but also an image of the future — a future in which a person constantly lives within “extensions,” whether they are technologies, social constructs, or even ideas without which it has already become difficult to move.

And maybe the exoskeleton is not about “iron on the body” at all, but about the exoskeleton of the soul: everything we create around ourselves to withstand the pressure of life. For some, it is faith — an invisible support that holds them up when their own strength is running out. For others, it is the sense of something greater than ourselves, something that gives direction to our steps and makes them part of a larger story.

Then the exoskeleton turns out not to be armor, but an extension — a way to keep moving forward, preserving the self and finding support in what lies beyond us.

• Erika Kolobanova — director and photographer
• Konstantins Belihs — multidisciplinary artist

jaNuSauleRitNeuzaust.title: Tvērums #11 “East”: What If the Sun Doesn’t Rise Tomorrow
jaNuSauleRitNeuzaust.shortDescription: Launch of Tvērums #11 “East”: a collective invocation of the Sun, with feasting and dancing through the night until dawn. DJ Henrijs Laķis. Venue: RAA, Matīsa Street 8.
jaNuSauleRitNeuzaust.description:
The sun rises in the east. But just because it always has doesn’t guarantee it will tomorrow. Conclusions about the future based on past experience are only possibly true — as David Hume argued. So at that moment of the year, before night grows longer than day, we invite you to help take care of the next sunrise.

On September 18 from 19:00, as we launch Tvērums issue 11, “East”, we will gather to call in the Sun. In the mood of the equinox and the start of astronomical autumn, we’ll also salute the almost sold-out “Magic” issue and bid farewell to a not-so-sunny summer, trying to conjure its kindness for the road ahead. After that — feasting and celebration through the night, waiting for a dawn that cannot be predicted with certainty. DJ Henrijs Laķis will provide music for dancing, and the brewery Labietis will help set the convivial tone.

Opening venue: RAA, the cultural space at the Veldze creative factory (Matīsa Street 8).

Illustration by Paula Betija Antenišķe.

The magazine is supported by the State Culture Capital Foundation and the VV Foundation.

tautasDziesmas.title: Folk Songs
tautasDziesmas.shortDescription: A creative afternoon and evening at RAA of singing, writing, drawing, and sharing – an open and welcoming space to explore folk songs through music and creative expression.
tautasDziesmas.description:
Join us for a creative afternoon and evening of singing, writing, drawing, and sharing together. This is an open and welcoming space to explore folk songs through music and creative expression.

Date: 17.09.2025
Doors open: 16:00
Singing: 18:00–22:00
Venue: RAA, Matīsa Street 8

sujmasinuTehno.title: Sewing Machine Techno
sujmasinuTehno.shortDescription: A performance where women transform sewing into rhythmic techno – turning industrial noise and mechanical movements into music.
sujmasinuTehno.description:
“Sewing Machine Techno” is a performance where women transform the act of working at sewing machines into a rhythmic sound piece. Industrial noise and mechanical movements create a techno-like aesthetic, turning an everyday tool into a musical instrument. The performance explores the relationship between labor, body, and sound – how monotonous rhythm can become art, how routine turns into ritual, and physical work transforms into a shared manifestation of energy and freedom.

Special guests:
+ BmwAudi (dj)
+ OtRa PUssYTe (dj)

dzoelaTeilore.title: Joelle Taylor Reading
dzoelaTeilore.shortDescription: As part of Queer Poetry Days, British poet, performer, and playwright Joelle Taylor will give a guest reading at RAA on July 8. The event will be in English with selected Latvian translations. Free entry.
dzoelaTeilore.description:
As part of Queer Poetry Days, on Monday, July 8, at 19:00, RAA will host the remarkable, passionate, and dynamic British poet Joelle Taylor.

Joelle Taylor is a poet, performer, and playwright. She is the author of several poetry collections, plays, and one novel. Her work has earned numerous literary awards, including the prestigious T. S. Eliot Prize for her collection "C+nto & Othered Poems" (2021). She has been the UK Slam Poetry Champion and is the founder of SLAMbassadors, the UK’s national youth slam championship.

Selections of Joelle Taylor’s poems in Latvian translation by Raimonda Arāja are available in the literary magazine "Punctum": https://www.punctummagazine.lv/2024/01/22/canto/

The event will be held in English, with selected Latvian translations of poems performed by Inga Gaile and Raimonda Arāja.

Free entry.

Organised by the association "Latvijas PEN". Joelle Taylor’s visit to Riga is supported by the British Embassy in Riga (@britishembassyriga).

Illustration by Elīna Brasliņa.

pilseetasNaktii.title: Cities by Night
pilseetasNaktii.shortDescription: Homo Novus presents the exhibition “Cities by Night” by Italian artist Valentina Medda, exploring women’s perceptions of safety and danger in urban space.
pilseetasNaktii.externalLinkText: Please Visit the Event Page on the Homo Novus website
pilseetasNaktii.description:
Cities by Night
Valentina Medda (Italy)

Cities by Night is a site-specific participatory project on women’s* perception of danger in the urban space.

In April, women of different backgrounds, origins, gender and ages were invited to explore the streets of Riga, wandering alone at dusk, avoiding the areas where they felt uncomfortable. The result of their walks are several “Riga by Night” maps of the city whose boundaries have been redrawn based on their experiences, with areas perceived as unsafe obscured by the artist, as if non-existing.

Ostensibly refiguring a merely personal experience, the new borders – geographical, physical, emotional and political – show how not only gender but also shared beliefs, prejudices and cultural background shape, redefine and contract urban space and someone’s access to it.

The project has so far been realized in Paris (FR), Amsterdam (NL), Bologna and Bergamo (IT), Trondheim (NO), Pristina (XZ), and Milan (IT).

Valentina Medda is a Sardinian interdisciplinary artist based in Bologna. Her artistic practice unfolds between image, performance and site-specific interventions, investigating the relationship between public and private, body and architecture, city and social belonging. Her work has been presented in international contexts including Santarcangelo Festival, AUAWIRLEBEN in Hamburg, Little Bastard in Norway, Mladi Levi in Ljubljana. She has been artist-in-residence at BAR in Beirut, Cité des Arts, Paris, Flux Factory, NY, Les bains connective, Brussels, MaisonVentidue, Bologna. She has received the Cimetta Fund, Movin up Regione Emilia Romagna, NYFA’s IAP Mentorship and Tina Art PRIZE. She was a winner of the distribution grant PERFORM EUROPE 2021, Stronger Peripheries-Southern Coalition and the 11th Italian Council. With her last project “The Last Lamentation” she won again the 2024/2025 edition of PERFORM EUROPE.

laimePliistUzTraukiem.title: LAIME PLĪST UZ TRAUKIEM
laimePliistUzTraukiem.shortDescription: A joint exhibition by Elza Albiņa and Uģis Albiņš exploring coexistence, dissolving boundaries, and the transformations of the everyday through installations and spatial narratives.
laimePliistUzTraukiem.when:
Exhibition opening on 28.08.2025 at 19:00
Open for viewing 29.08.–31.08. from 16:00 to 19:00
laimePliistUzTraukiem.description:
We are delighted to announce and invite you to the joint exhibition by Elza Albiņa (@elzzala) and Uģis Albiņš (@ug_gis): **LAIME PLĪST UZ TRAUKIEM**.

**Opening:** 28.08. at 19:00
**Exhibition on view:** 29.08. to 31.08., from 16:00 to 19:00

**Venue:**
Matīsa Street 8
@raa_riga
@veldze.radosarupnica

The exhibition presents an installation — a series of objects created by Elza and Uģis — forming a shared-life landscape, a spatial reflection on coexistence, breaking down boundaries, and the transformations of the everyday.

**About Elza Albiņa:**
Elza creates audiovisual works and installations. In her artistic practice, she often integrates techniques and technologies drawn from her background in restoration, offering new forms of interpretation for events beyond the boundaries of facts. She explores the ambivalent relationships between fragments and wholes, copies and originals. Elza holds a Bachelor's degree in Kustība.Attēls.Skaņa and a Master's degree in Visual Communication from the Art Academy of Latvia. She actively participates in exhibitions and art events in Latvia and abroad.

**About Uģis Albiņš:**
Uģis creates works that reflect and provoke multilayered questions about our surrounding environment, using various techniques and media, including 3D printing, to reveal dialogues between material and form. He works with systems and standards that shape our culture, embodying specific aesthetic qualities. Uģis approaches abstract questions as visual phenomena, challenging the meaning of form beyond its utilitarian function and using function itself as a mode of expression and part of the visual language. He holds both a Bachelor's and a Master's degree in Visual Communication from the Art Academy of Latvia and actively participates in exhibitions and art events in Latvia and internationally.

secondVarietyShow.title: Second Variety Show
secondVarietyShow.shortDescription: Join us for the Second Variety Show at Kàtoptron Teātris on September 1st, 2025 – an evening of performances, musical improvisation, and playful experiments.
secondVarietyShow.price: Entrance fee: 4€ (for performers and Theater). Donations welcome.
secondVarietyShow.description:
You are welcome to participate in our terrible second variety show at Kàtoptron Teātris, on September 1st, the year of 2025 (relatively speaking), at RADOŠĀ RŪPNĪCĀ VELDZE.

This time, we continue our previous performative activities but with a twist. Borrowing from Pepsi.

At 19:00 you are invited to spectate two brand new mini performances by Santa Didzus, directed by Santa Didzus and Helias Dzimoulakis. Following the controversial success of her previous performances, Santa strikes back with wrath and might. Make sure you witness our favourite puppeteer/actor in (fr)action.

At 20:00, we continue the fan-favourite Musical Oracle Series. How does it work? You are invited to enter the Kàtoptron Theatre room by yourself, sit on a chair and close your eyes. Our musicians, led by the High Priestess Anna Ozoliņa, will "read" you and attempt to create your sound portrait through instant improvisation. In a way, you're invited to listen to a tune made exclusively for you that will be heard only once in your lifetime. Due to its mystical and intimate nature, any type of recording is not allowed.

At 21:00, you're invited to bring your own musical – or non-musical – instrument and join an improvisational jam led and championed by our unorthodox mime, Mr Mish. Feel free to open up, fail, create magical cringe or even be absorbed by our circus's hypnotic oblivion.

In the meantime, Daniels will be teaching you how to juggle, and other bizarre characters would love to provide you some insight into their world.

In case you wanna book your attendance for the Musical Oracle and the double performance, please contact us on Instagram: Kàtoptron_Teatris, Facebook: Helias Dzimoulakis, at eliastzimoulakis@gmail.com or enter@raa.space.

Entrance Fee for the whole event: 4€ (for performers and Theater).
Donations are happily accepted.
Drinks are provided by the Theater.

kabiirijasSalons1.title: Kabiirijas Salons 1
kabiirijasSalons1.shortDescription: Every Thursday of the week the Kàtoptron Teātris (Matīsa 8, RAA) will be open to all -failed or almost failed - artists in Rīga, for free and experimental artistic expression of any kind. From painting and sketching, to open music Jamming, performance, grotesque fashion, dance, poetry etc.
kabiirijasSalons1.description:
Professional and non professional artists are welcome to take a seat in our cozy and wonderful living room, fix their own drink and make Art. Or, not make Art at all; it's called voyeurism.

How does it work? Let me explain by removing the garlic from my soul first. Okay, now: Doors are open from 20:30, where you are able to join us, carrying an idea that you would like to bring to life. Anything. Anything that can make your life more interesting. I violently encourage you to come, sit, drink, sketch, write, perform for yourself or others through improvisation. Play collective music that can explode some mysticism among us; dance to it, cry to it, fart to it or, snob it. Draw, draw on bodies, on paper, on foreheads. Play music, or destroy it. Perform Magic or Rituals; make fun of magic and rituals. Lie down and dream, inside our own tiny theater stage (that makes every micropimpis feel like a horse).

You can be as flirty as you want to, and touch only when there's consent; otherwise I'll cut your dick and feed it to our imaginary dog, Helga.

The failed Metaphor is the new, mightier Metaphor. Let's try vandalizing it, yet with charm.

IMPORTANT: Our theater provides some music instruments (one electric guitar with amplifier, one classical guitar, a tiny set of drums, a 19th Century table piano), we have markers, dolls, lights, even some theatrical clothes. In case you have something specific in mind, lūdzu bring it yourself (for example, your own instrument, something pathetic preferably).

Thursday, August 14th: Dialogo ad Absurdum, Open stage Freak show, music playing, magical symbol creation and body drawing, awkwardness, and hopefully a film.

Entrance Free, But DONATE, so we keep on

shelfStories.title: RAA Museum: Shelf Stories
shelfStories.shortDescription: A Museum of objects sitting unmoved in a shelf corner whilst life is taking place around them. When have they last been moved?
shelfStories.description:
A Museum of objects sitting unmoved in a shelf corner whilst life is taking place around them. When have they last been moved?

Each object on a shelf has a time when it was last moved, touched or used. After that they have been biding their time. Shelf objects have this thing in common, because they are mostly inert and the shelf is the place where they can be just that. There might be a time for them to be picked up again or to move due to the forces of nature. There most certainly will be this time. Each such object hence has a natural life cycle that sees them being placed there for lack of autonomy and them being replaced or taken away. But why were they placed there? Such a momentous instance has a circumstance, has an intent, has some one who is doing the action. Does that someone know of the object's predicament. That it would be the last time for this object to move in a loong time. Here we present the photo-album of these objects during their life time.

If you have photos of objects in your shelf, please bring them or their photos to RAA and tell us the story of the object. What brought the object there, what happened on that day to cast the destiny of the object. It will go on exhibit on the Thursday 14th of August at 19:00.

saulesGaismasMekleejumos.title: Saules Gaismas Mekleejumos
saulesGaismasMekleejumos.shortDescription: Saules gaismas meklējumos /// In search of sunshine. Solo Show by Fricis Ripa.

fireWorkshop.title: Fire Workshop with Arta Raituma
fireWorkshop.shortDescription: Arta Raituma introduces participants to the transformative element of fire through an experimental wood-burning technique.
fireWorkshop.description:
Fire is an element of transformation, refining what it encounters. The artist will introduce participants to this playful and seductive element that is the core principle of her artistic practice.

Participants are invited to learn an experimentally developed wood burning technique that creates a hazy, nuanced visual effect using the symbiosis of flame and wooden surface. The poetic contradiction between fire and wood suggests a memory of landscape, hence people are invited to explore their ‘inner landscapes’, to learn a natural and self-empowering expression as well as get to know more about sustainable art approaches.

All materials for the workshop are provided. The workshop is suitable for people without any previous knowledge in visual arts as well as art students or professional artists who would like to expand their skills and possibly implement new techniques in their own practice.

The length of the workshop is around 2.5 hours.

fireExhibition.title: Fire Workshop Exhibition
fireExhibition.shortDescription: An exhibition showcasing artworks created during Arta Raituma's fire workshop.
fireExhibition.description: An exhibition featuring works created during Arta Raituma’s fire workshop. Explore unique wood-burned artworks that reflect the interplay of fire and surface, capturing memory and transformation.

theatreOpening.shortDescription: Opening of the Katoptron Teātris. Kino and Puppet Theatre starting from 19:00. Program: Reversed Oracle Series -- Open Stage Gringefest -- Horrible Puppett -- Radio Fake Magic -- Juggling Workshop.

kasAugStarpMums.title: Kas Aug Starp Mums
kasAugStarpMums.shortDescription: Ksenija Kumačova (b. 1997) is a young photography artist who actively explores the interaction between humans and local nature in her practice. Her interest in plants began eight years ago during her pharmaceutical studies, when plant research required a scientific approach. This rational inquiry soon evolved into a broader interest — viewing plants through the lens of culture, art, and emotional self-reflection.
kasAugStarpMums.description:
Ksenija Kumačova (b. 1997) is a young photography artist who actively explores the interaction between humans and local nature in her practice. Her interest in plants began eight years ago during her pharmaceutical studies, when plant research required a scientific approach. This rational inquiry soon evolved into a broader interest — viewing plants through the lens of culture, art, and emotional self-reflection.

Photographer: Ksenija Kumačova (@ksenijakum) 
Curator: Jana Zariņa (@tobeawhile) 
07.08. 19.00–22:00 (atklāšana)

dzejaSinusoida.title: Poetry: Sinusoid
dzejaSinusoida.shortDescription: A poetry marathon with unexpected twists by the group “Syntax Stupor,” with music by Dabsolis and Kaspars Sils.
dzejaSinusoida.description:
A poetry marathon with unexpected twists by the poetry enthusiasts’ group “Syntax Stupor.”

Participants:
Laima Ābele, Andris Alps, Valts Kleins, Kristīne Juckoviča, Zane Brūvere-Kvēpa, Mārīte Atēna, Elza Dobičina, Ilze Kuzmicka, Dita Putniņa, Aļesja Loseva, Arnolds Ščuckis, Pauls Kauķis, Meldra Ķemere, Linda Tīmane, Marta Veismane, Elīna Līce and others.

The musical atmosphere will be provided by Dabsolis and Kaspars Sils.

P.S. An open microphone will be available during the event.

tavaasKurpees.title: In Your Shoes
tavaasKurpees.shortDescription: We invite you to the group exhibition of young artists “In Your Shoes” at RAA – the newest art space in Riga!
tavaasKurpees.when: 11 July, opening at 19:00. Until 18 July, every day from 16:00 to 19:00

raaOpening.title: R A A opening #1
raaOpening.shortDescription: The new interdisciplinary cultural space R A A will open its doors.
raaOpening.when: Friday 11th of July from 6pm magno cum tempore till 11pm
raaOpening.description:
On Friday, July 11, at 6:00 PM magno cum tempore, the new interdisciplinary cultural space R A A will open its doors. The opening evening will feature: an exhibition by young artists from the Art Academy of Latvia under the guidance of Maija Kurševa, electronic and acoustic music, exploration of the space, conversations with sparkling drinks, as well as a theatrical performance by the makers of the cultural space, marking the beginning of R A A's theatrical activities.

R A A opening #1

Program
18.00 R A A Opening magno cum tempore (Dj Meile)
19.00 Opening of the LMA’s exhibition of young artists “In your shoes” (within the festival SABRS, organised by Maija Kurševa)
20.00 Musical performance (Katrīna Kovaļuka, Ēriks Frišenfelds)
21.00 Theatrical opening (Helias Dzimoulakis, Leonhard Horstmeyer)
22.00 Live sound (Jana Zariņa, Sarma Gabrena)
